title: Region of interest signaling for streaming three-dimensional video information
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure describe methods and apparatuses for region of interest signaling for streaming three-dimensional video information.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. One or more computer-readable media having instructions that, when executed, cause a device to: transmit, to a remote device, an indication of a projection format supported by the device, the projection format to be used to map captured three-dimensional video to two- dimensional video; receive, in a real-time transport control protocol ("RTCP") feedback message, an indication of a region of interest ("ROI") from the remote device, the ROI being associated with a first region of a plurality of regions of a two-dimensional video image that represents a three-dimensional video image; create a regional two-dimensional video image from the three-dimensional video image based on the projection format and the ROI; and encode the regional two-dimensional video image for transmission to the remote device. 2. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the ROI is a first ROI and the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to: transmit a list of a plurality of available ROIs to the remote device, wherein the remote device is to select the first ROI from the plurality of available ROIs. 3. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 2, wherein the indication of the first ROI is a descriptor that corresponds to the list of the plurality of available ROIs. 4. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the indication of the ROI is to include one or more position values to identify a corner position of the first region within the two-dimensional video image and one or more size values to identify a size of the first region. 5. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the device is further to transmit or receive an indication of an ability to signal ROI via RTCP in an exchange of session description protocol ("SDP") offer and answer messages. 6. The one or more computer-readable media of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to: transmit the indication of the projection format in a session description protocol ("SDP") offer message. 7. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 6, wherein the SDP offer message includes a plurality of projection formats supported by the device. 8. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 7, wherein the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to: receive an SDP answer message that includes a selection of the projection format from the plurality of projection formats. 9. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 1, wherein the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to: encode an ROI indicator associated with the ROI for transmission to the remote device along with the encoded two-dimensional video image. 10. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 9, wherein the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to transmit the ROI indicator in a real-time transport protocol ("RTP") header extension and to encode the two-dimensional video image in RTP packets. 11. An apparatus having circuitry to: receive a list of one or more projection formats supported by a remote device, the one or more projection formats to be used to map captured three-dimensional video to two- dimensional video; select a first projection format from the list and transmit an indication of the first projection format to the remote device; transmit, in a real-time transport control protocol ("RTCP") feedback message, an indication of a region of interest ("ROI") associated with a first region of a plurality of regions of a two-dimensional video image that represents a three-dimensional video image; and receive one or more real-time transport protocol ("RTP") packets including encoded video data that corresponds to the ROI. 12. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the circuitry is further to: receive a list of a plurality of available ROIs from the remote device; and select the ROI from the list of the plurality of available ROIs. 13. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the circuitry is further to select the ROI without prior indication from the remote device of available ROIs, wherein the indication of the ROI is to include one or more position values to identify a corner position of the first region within the two-dimensional video image and one or more size values to identify a size of the first region. 14. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the circuitry is to receive the list of one or more projection formats in a session description protocol ("SDP") offer message. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the circuitry is to transmit the indication of the first projection format in an SDP answer message. 16. The apparatus of any one of claims 11-15, wherein the circuitry comprises a multimedia telephony over Internet protocol multimedia subsystem ("MTSI") client. 17. One or more computer-readable media having instructions that, when executed, cause a device to: receive, in a real-time transport control protocol ("RTCP") feedback message, an indication of a region of interest ("ROI") from a remote device, the indication of the ROI to identify a first region of a plurality of regions of a three-dimensional video image; create a regional two-dimensional video image from the three-dimensional video image based on the indication of the ROI; and encode the regional two-dimensional video image for transmission to the remote device. 18. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 17, wherein the ROI is a first ROI and the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to: transmit a list of a plurality of available ROIs using a session description protocol ("SDP") to the remote device, wherein the remote device is to select the first ROI from the plurality 

 of available ROIs and the indication of each available ROI is to include spherical coordinate information to identify a corresponding region. 19. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 18, wherein the indication of the first ROI is a descriptor that corresponds to the list of the plurality of available ROIs. 20. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 17, wherein the indication of the ROI is to include spherical coordinate information to identify the first region. 21. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 18 or 20, wherein the spherical coordinate information includes a yaw angle of a center of the first region, a pitch angle of the center of the first region, an angular height and width of the first region, a horizontal position of a top-left corner of the first region, a starting and ending pitch angle of the first region, or a starting and ending yaw angle of the first region. 22. The one or more computer-readable media of any one of claims 17-20, wherein an indication of an ability to signal ROI via RTCP is to be communicated in an exchange of session description protocol ("SDP") offer and answer messages. 23. The one or more computer-readable media of any one of claims 17-20, wherein the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to encode an ROI indicator associated with the ROI for transmission to the remote device along with the encoded regional two-dimensional video image. 24. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 23, wherein the instructions, when executed, further cause the device to transmit the ROI indicator in a real-time transport protocol ("RTP") header extension and to encode the regional two-dimensional video image in one or more RTP packets. 25. The one or more computer-readable media of claim 24, wherein the ROI indicator is to include the spherical coordinate information in a session description protocol ("SDP") offer or answer message.
